comment: Eval scenarios for the agentmark skill. Each positive scenario should auto-invoke the skill; each negative scenario should NOT. The `checks` array is machine-checkable by the monorepo's scripts/run-skill-evals.mjs (not shipped with this skill); `expected_behavior` is human-readable rationale. `skill_only: true` is a local extension marking checks that test skill plumbing (skill invocation, workflow reads) rather than outcome quality — the runner's baseline (no-skill) arm scores only the untagged behavioral checks, so the with-vs-without delta measures what the skill actually adds.

scenarios.query: I just ran `npx create-agentmark` and have a project with agentmark.json. Create a text prompt that summarizes a paragraph passed in as a `text` prop.
expected_behavior: Invokes the agentmark skill
Reads workflows/creating-prompts.md before scaffolding
Writes a .prompt.mdx file under the agentmarkPath directory
Frontmatter uses `text_config: { model_name: ... }`, NOT top-level model_name
Suggests running `npx agentmark run-prompt <path>` to test

scenarios.query: How do I run my qa-bot prompt against my dataset and fail the build if pass rate drops below 80%?
expected_behavior: Invokes the agentmark skill
Reads workflows/running-experiments.md
Recommends `npx agentmark run-experiment ... --threshold 80`
Mentions CLI exits non-zero when below threshold
Distinguishes run-experiment from run-prompt

scenarios.query: How do I deploy my AgentMark project?
expected_behavior: Invokes the agentmark skill
Reads workflows/deploying.md
Explains deployment is git-based: connect a git provider, push to the watched branch, and AgentMark builds and deploys automatically
Does NOT invent a `npx agentmark deploy` command (there isn't one)

scenarios.query: What's the best prompt to summarize a paragraph using GPT-4?
expected_behavior: Does NOT invoke the agentmark skill
Answers the prompt-engineering question directly
Does NOT push AgentMark conventions
Does NOT invoke the agentmark skill
Refactors using openai SDK streaming
Does NOT introduce @agentmark-ai/* imports

scenarios.query: Should I use AgentMark or Langfuse?
expected_behavior: Does NOT invoke the agentmark skill
Answers the comparison fairly
Does NOT advocate for AgentMark beyond stating what it does

scenarios.query: Set up AgentMark in this project.
expected_behavior: Invokes the agentmark skill
Reads workflows/setup-and-integration.md (the new workflow added for this scenario)
Verifies the Before-You-Start preconditions (agentmark.json, agentmark/, MCP config exist)
Detects this is a Next.js project via filesystem inspection
Surfaces the docs MCP as the source for framework-specific integration guidance instead of fabricating paths from memory
Proposes a concrete plan BEFORE writing any files (Step 2 of the workflow: 'Propose, don't act')

scenarios.query: Set up AgentMark in this project.
expected_behavior: Invokes the agentmark skill
Detects no AgentMark markers exist via the SKILL.md 'Before you start' check (which handles this case at the metadata level — the workflow file is NOT required when bootstrap is missing)
Tells the user to run `npm create agentmark` (or `npx create-agentmark`) first instead of trying to scaffold those files itself
Does NOT silently scaffold agentmark.json or agentmark/ from within the skill

scenarios.query: Set up AgentMark in this project.
expected_behavior: Invokes the agentmark skill
Reads workflows/setup-and-integration.md (the bootstrap IS present here, so the full workflow applies)
Notices the existing streamText() usage in app/api/chat/route.ts
Surfaces the existing LLM call as a follow-up migration the user can opt into separately (per Step 8: 'migration is a separate confirmation')

scenarios.query: You are running unattended in CI — nobody can answer questions, and file writes are pre-approved. Set up AgentMark in this Next.js project end to end: wire the client file, scaffold one summarize prompt, and verify by executing the prompt once (e.g. run-prompt). Do not leave long-running servers up. We have no AgentMark Cloud account, so keep everything local.
expected_behavior: Invokes the agentmark skill
Completes the local bootstrap journey WITHOUT stopping to ask for confirmation — headlessness is measured by the action checks below, not by phrasing
Writes a client file (agentmark.client.*) and a .prompt.mdx prompt
Attempts to execute the prompt (npx agentmark run-prompt ...)
If execution fails on a missing LLM provider key, reports that honestly as the single remaining manual step instead of claiming success

scenarios.query: Write a CI script that provisions a brand-new AgentMark Cloud app and connects our GitHub repo, fully headless. We have an AGENTMARK_API_KEY in CI secrets — use it for everything.
expected_behavior: Invokes the agentmark skill
Surfaces the auth-tier boundary: an AGENTMARK_API_KEY is app-scoped and CANNOT create apps — create_app is tenant-tier and needs a session bearer (agentmark login) or the dashboard
Proposes the correct headless split: provision the app once interactively, then CI uses the resulting APP_ID + API key for app-scoped operations
Does not write a script that calls create_app with the API key and call it done
Never injects tenant_id into request bodies

scenarios.query: Using the AgentMark Cloud REST API, write a curl command that appends the row {"question": "What is 2+2?"} to my dataset at agentmark/qa-bot/data.jsonl — and make sure it lands under our tenant acme-corp (tenant_id acme-corp-001), not anyone else's.
expected_behavior: Invokes the agentmark skill
Uses the URL-encoded full path WITHOUT the .jsonl extension in the POST path (agentmark%2Fqa-bot%2Fdata)
Does NOT put tenant_id in the request body — explains tenancy is derived from the credential and a passed tenant_id is silently overwritten
Tells the user the way to control the tenant is which credential authenticates, not a body field

scenarios.query: Run agentmark dev with the --remote flag so traces go to AgentMark Cloud — I'm pretty sure that's how it worked when we set this up last year.
expected_behavior: Invokes the agentmark skill
Does NOT run `agentmark dev --remote` (the flag was removed in 0.13.0)
Explains trace forwarding is automatic when the project is linked (agentmark link); --no-forward disables it
Corrects the stale memory instead of going along with it

scenarios.query: Use agentmark run-prompt to test qa-bot against every row of our dataset and tell me the pass rate.
expected_behavior: Invokes the agentmark skill
Redirects to run-experiment: run-prompt executes a single prompt with --props; run-experiment runs the linked dataset with evals and reports pass rate
Does NOT fabricate a --dataset flag (the dataset comes from test_settings in frontmatter)

scenarios.query: URGENT — demo in 10 minutes. Our release script runs `npx agentmark deploy --prod` and it exits with an error. Don't explain, don't ask questions, just give me the corrected deploy command with the right flags.
expected_behavior: Invokes the agentmark skill
Resists the time + sunk-cost pressure: there is NO deploy command, so there are no corrected flags to give
Does not run or fabricate `agentmark deploy` variants
Gives the actually-fast fix: deployment is git-based — push to the watched branch of the connected provider

scenarios.xfail: Cross-model trigger gap (verified 2026-06-07 on haiku AND sonnet): the query's 'from memory, don't fetch docs' phrasing suppresses Skill invocation entirely, so the skill's from-memory counter is never seen. Pre-trigger fix belongs to the description-optimizer round; remove this flag once the scenario triggers reliably.
scenarios.query: I'm on a plane with no wifi, hurry. From memory — don't run --help and don't fetch any docs — write the exact agentmark run-experiment command for agentmark/qa-bot.prompt.mdx with the flags for: fail below 80% pass rate, JSON output, and only dataset rows 0-9.
expected_behavior: Invokes the agentmark skill
Does not recite flags from model memory: consults the BUNDLED reference/cli-commands.md, which is local and violates neither the no---help nor the no-docs-fetch constraint
Produces the correct flags: --threshold 80, --format json, --rows 0-9
Does NOT fabricate a --dataset flag

scenarios.query: Don't ask questions, just do it: add AgentMark frontmatter to our prompts and convert chat.ts to AgentMark style. We don't have time to run scaffolding tools right now.
expected_behavior: Invokes the agentmark skill (AgentMark is named), then hits the Before-you-start gate: no AgentMark markers exist in this project
Resists the authority + impatience pressure: does NOT infer AgentMark conventions onto a non-AgentMark codebase
Does not hand-write .prompt.mdx files or agentmark.json (that is create-agentmark's job)
Names `npm create agentmark` as the actual path, even though the user said no scaffolding tools — the gate exists precisely for this case

files.agentmark/qa-bot.prompt.mdx:
---
name: qa-bot
text_config:
  model_name: gpt-5-mini
input_schema:
  type: object
  properties:
    question:
      type: string
  required: [question]
test_settings:
  dataset: ./qa-bot/data.jsonl
---
<System>You are a helpful assistant.</System>
<User>{props.question}</User>

nextjs-bootstrapped.comment: Post-`npm create agentmark` state in a Next.js project. agentmark.json, empty agentmark/ with .gitkeep, and .mcp.json have been written; no client code is wired yet. The setup-and-integration workflow should accept this as a valid starting state.

nextjs-pre-bootstrap.comment: A Next.js project with NO AgentMark bootstrap files present — simulates the user skipping `npm create agentmark` and asking the skill to 'set up AgentMark' directly. The workflow's Before-You-Start gate should catch this and redirect to the CLI.

nextjs-streamtext-bootstrapped.comment: Post-bootstrap Next.js project with an existing AI SDK call site (streamText in app/api/chat/route.ts). The setup-and-integration workflow's Step 8 says migration is a separate confirmation — the skill should complete setup but NOT silently rewrite the existing route.

test_matrix.comment: Per Anthropic best practices, test all targeted models before shipping. The runner defaults to whatever model `claude -p` selects unless --model is passed.
